name: ScrollCaptureCallback
platform: Android platform API; introduced in Android 12 / API 31
license: [uncertain] Android platform API; AOSP source is Apache 2.0, but the runtime API is distributed as part of Android
version: [uncertain] Current Android platform API (docs current as of 2026)
project_status: [uncertain] Actively maintained callback API used by Android scroll capture
capture_driver: Callback-based cooperative rendering. The system searches for a target, starts a session, requests specific capture rectangles, and the app renders those regions into the provided Surface
compose_support: Used indirectly by Compose 1.7+ internals on Android 12+, but it is not a Compose-first API
view_support: Custom View or ViewGroup implementations, window-level callbacks, and platform-provided helpers for standard scrollable widgets
stitching_strategy: Native tile-based incremental capture assembled by the screenshot host
matching_algorithm: None; the callback works in content coordinates instead of image matching
scroll_offset_trust: The implementation is expected to track scroll delta during capture so requested captureArea values map to the correct content
duplicate_frame_handling: Generally unnecessary when the implementation can render offscreen directly; otherwise the app can avoid redundant moves itself
seam_selection_policy: Coordinate-based exact region placement; the callback returns the actual captured Rect
confidence_fallback: The callback may return a partial Rect or an empty Rect when content is unavailable or out of bounds
static_chrome_handling: Good. The callback defines scrollBounds so only content that moves with scrolling should be captured
dynamic_content_robustness: Better than screenshot stitching, but content that changes during session execution can still create inconsistencies between requests
content_type_fitness: Strong for custom scroll containers that can render stable content regions on demand
memory_strategy: Implementation-defined, but designed for partial or offscreen rendering into a session Surface rather than full giant bitmap capture
stitching_speed: Efficient incremental path when implemented well
max_output_constraints: [uncertain] Limited by platform session buffers, Surface throughput, device memory, and screenshot-host limits
frame_interval: System-driven on demand per onScrollCaptureImageRequest call
output_format: Rendered image buffers to Surface; final saved format is chosen by the screenshot host
output_quality: Native render quality of the callback implementation; final compression depends on the host export path
oem_compatibility: [uncertain] Host behavior and screenshot UX can vary by OEM even though the callback contract is platform-defined
integration: Implement android.view.ScrollCaptureCallback and attach it with View.setScrollCaptureCallback or Window.registerScrollCaptureCallback
media_projection_compliance: Not applicable; this callback is part of ScrollCapture, not MediaProjection
windowing_behavior: Captures content within the containing view or registered window coordinate space
permissions_required: No special dangerous permission for supporting the callback
chart_stitching_fitness: High when the custom view can render chart regions deterministically on request
chat_stitching_fitness: High when the custom view can map scroll position to content deterministically and restore original state at session end
